title: Piquant Smoky Greek Feta Tiropita
description: A spicy and creamy Greek dish featuring oat milk and smoked feta, perfect for a show-stopping snack or appetizer, and is Jain-friendly as it contains no animal products except feta which can be substituted with a Jain alternative

ingredients.name: smoked feta cheese
ingredients.notes: crumbly, can be substituted with Jain alternative

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the oat milk, smoked feta cheese, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper until well combined.
Unroll the filo dough and cut it into 4 equal pieces.
Brush each piece of filo dough with olive oil and spoon the feta mixture onto one end of each piece, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the filo dough over the filling to form a triangle and press the edges together to seal.
Place the tiropita on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the tops with olive oil.
Bake the tiropita for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove the tiropita from the oven and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
Serve the tiropita warm and enjoy.
